extraction.one_line_summary: LUTSENKO Alexey won from the early 8-rider breakaway, surviving solo after driving the pace throughout, while ALAPHILIPPE Julian showed symbolic revenge attack after time penalty.
tactical_patterns: VAN AVERMAET Greg gained over 3:17 on GC from breakaway, demonstrating CCC's desperation tactics for sponsor visibility.
POWLESS Neilson launched the key climb attack that split the break, yet blew up to 4th—aggressive timing cost the stage.
ALAPHILIPPE Julian's futile late attack for 2 seconds reveals emotional rather than tactical decision-making after 20-second penalty acceptance.

rider_signals.LUTSENKO Alexey: positive: drove breakaway pace all day then soloed to victory, showing superior diesel engine over punchier riders
rider_signals.POWLESS Neilson: positive: 4th place at 2:17 on 24th birthday in debut Tour mountain finish after initiating winning move
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: positive: 3rd at 2:15, gained massive GC time for struggling CCC team seeking sponsorship
rider_signals.HERRADA Jesús: neutral: 2nd at 51 seconds in 7th Tour but still no stage win, chronic nearly-man syndrome
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: 5th with symbolic attack after penalty, honor over tactics but no GC damage
rider_signals.YATES Adam: positive: top 10 finish maintains 3-second yellow jersey gap, defended efficiently without unnecessary effort
